I started work at 8:00 AM and finished at 6:00 PM yesterday. That's 10 hours in total. Hmm, but my performance was only average. Maybe there are better times to be on the road? Sometimes the rush hours can be too busy, making it hard to complete orders quickly or find good routes.

I remember reading that peak hours like lunch and dinner times have more orders, but also more riders, so competition is high. Maybe if I adjust my working hours a bit, I could avoid some of the congestion and grab more orders when there's less competition.

What time do most people order food? Probably around 12 PM for lunch and 6-8 PM for dinner. If I work during these times, maybe I can get more orders. But if too many riders are online then, it might be harder to get accepted into those orders.

Alternatively, working slightly before the peak hours could help me grab orders as people start ordering without the rush. Or maybe extending my hours a bit beyond the usual 8 AM to 6 PM could allow me to cover more shifts when others aren't available.

Maybe if I start a bit later, say 9 AM, I can catch the lunch rush when others might be taking breaks or have already delivered some orders.

Another angle: looking at when others finish work. If many riders finish at 6 PM, there might be a drop in available drivers right after, leading to more orders but fewer people to take them. Alternatively, if I start earlier at 7 AM, I might get the first wave of orders when others aren't online yet, but that's probably not common since most people start around 8 AM. Maybe starting at 7:30 AM could help me get a head start.

But based on yesterday's stats, I was in the middle range, so perhaps tweaking my hours to align more with peak demand times when others are not as available would be better.

Alternatively, maybe I need to work longer hours but be strategic about when. If I extend my working time by an hour or two after 6 PM, that could help me capture more orders when competition is lower.

Wait, another thought: if many riders finish at 6 PM, the system might prioritize those who are still online for the evening rush. So maybe ending later gives me a better chance of being selected for more orders.

So considering all this, perhaps I should start earlier to catch some early orders and end later to capture the dinner rush when competition is lower.

So, considering all this, maybe shifting my start time a bit later or ending earlier could help, but that might reduce my total working hours and thus potential earnings. Alternatively, extending the end time by an hour or two to catch the later orders.

I think a good balance would be to start at 8:00 AM as before but finish at 7 PM today. That way, I can cover more of the evening rush when others have finished their shifts, potentially getting more orders and higher earnings without too much competition.

But wait, is there a downside? Longer hours might lead to fatigue, which could affect my performance and safety on the road. Maybe taking breaks during the day or managing my energy better would be essential if I extend my working hours.
